title: Location of the Repository
text: Location of the Repository Every Uniface application uses paths to locate the tables or files associated with the entities it references. The starting path for every entity is taken from a set of default paths that were established during installation. The installation procedure creates: Default paths to each DBMS connector you installed Default paths to parts of the Repository One default path to the DBMS that you selected as the default DBMS for your entities When you start any Uniface application, you can use the default paths to locate entities, or you can use an assignment file, which contains assignments that redirect entities from default paths onto other paths. Default paths Entities of the Repository are located on the following paths: $SYS—for the PRATT.PRINTER entity $IDF—for the remaining entities (with the *.DICT suffix) By default, these paths are directed to an SQLite database which is automatically installed with Uniface. If you use a different DBMS as the Repository, you can direct these paths to that database using assignments. It is even possible to direct each path to a different database, although this is not typical. If one of these paths leads to a relational database such as SQLite, Sybase, or Oracle, the entities on that path are found in the associated DBMS. If one of these paths leads to a record-level DBMS, the *.DICT entities and PRATT.PRINTER are located in the current working directory. Related Links Paths and Path Assignments A path is a named route to a resource required by the application, such as a database connector and database, a remote server, or a file location. Assignment Files A Uniface assignment file contains one or more assignment settings, which determine the environment of a Uniface application and control aspects of its behavior. Assignment settings can determine the location of application resources and servers, set environment variables, define application logicals, and set Uniface system settings to influence runtime behavior. Assignment Settings An assignment setting is a setting or definition in an assignment file that determines one aspect of the behavior or environment of a Uniface application. Assignment settings can specify the location of application resources and servers, set environment variables, define application logicals, and set Uniface system settings to influence runtime behavior.
